title: Customize notifications when inviting users to boards

posts.author: straister (Shelly Traister)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Monday Community!

It would be super nice for us to be able to add users to boards without them automatically being notified.

We can (painfully) turn off their notifications at each and every user level (if the person subscribing them to the board has admin rights even), and then (painfully) add them back, but when you are working on a “plan”, and want to plot people into the plan and do some review and pre-work, before discussing it with them (OR sharing the board) - they may not even know they’ll be on the project, it is very silly to send them an invite TO said board.

It would be great if sending a notification during invitation (much like sharepoint let’s you deselect the email) could be OPTIONAL, thus allowing us to choose when the invite goes out by Monday (with less than perfect timing), or via a link in an email where we have control over the messaging.
Also, while I’m on that… would be REALLY nice if when we do use email (both subscribing to board, and inviting to Monday), if we could type a short message indicating context and supporting the invitation (especially helpful if we were to share outside our organization - which we’d like to do eventually - for real collab).

Our current workaround is to build a temporary text column, and plot names in it until the board is shared. This not only makes for extra work, but it means that we can’t utilize any dashboards/reporting elements that support resource utilization, because it’s no longer a people field.

EDIT (ADD) > A potential alternative option might be - Allow us to use people on boards they are not subscribed to, and even as I add this - I see value for internally working on management level organizational data where people fields add value, but can’t be used because the people are not part of the leadership group needing to see that data about them.

Anyone else struggle with this one?! Chime in… Thanks!

posts.author: cfraticelli (Celene Fraticelli)
posts.cookedHtml: I totally agree with this request!

posts.author: MikeH (Mike Heness)
posts.cookedHtml:
That would be really helpful and allow a more flexible approach to how we add people to boards:
1/ simple add and turn off notification
2/ personalised notification if needed

posts.author: JohnW (John Waller)
posts.cookedHtml:
straister:

but when you are working on a “plan”, and want to plot people into the plan and do some review and pre-work, before discussing it with them (OR sharing the board) - they may not even know they’ll be on the project , it is very silly to send them an invite TO said board.

We face this dilemma every time we create a board. Monday assumes everyone in the People column, with Notifications activated, needs to know immediately.

We often set up boards in draft mode for later review. Later we go “live”.

Currently we have to omit adding people so they don’t get false notifications or tell them to ignore the notification.

For us the immediate solution could be Draft Mode (no notifications) and Live (or Published) Mode for boards (everyone gets notified).

straister:

thus allowing us to choose when the invite goes out by Monday

That would be perfect for planning.

posts.author: straister (Shelly Traister)
posts.cookedHtml:
That’s a great approach @JohnW - the draft would prevent you from shutting off (pausing) automations related to notifications as well, which is another part of the issue here.

It would not solve the fundamental “let us use people fields without the people being subscribed to the board” issue, but it’s another really great function that would be helpful!!!

posts.author: JohnW (John Waller)
posts.cookedHtml:
straister:

the draft would prevent you from shutting off (pausing) automations related to notifications as well

Yep.

I’ve used “Draft/Live” features in other software. Works great.

Would be an excellent addition to Monday. Let’s see if they feel it’s compelling enough to add to the roadmap.

posts.author: JamesD (James Dunlop)
posts.cookedHtml:
Straitster, did you ever get a resolution to this? I can open a new chat to start the conversation again if you did not. I’m being asked by my executive team to use Monday.com, but the system does not work well for us while in the planning phase. We’re creating everything outside of Monday and communicating via emails with files attached to align the management team, then we integrate into Monday. We’d really like one system to create the plan, assign the plan, have management approve and then turn on the notifications to the team so they can log in and review.

The way we tried using Monday is by creating our plans in folder with boards, pulses, assignee’s and proposed due dates. The issue with Monday.com is that as soon as we outline a plan and assign a person, the person assigned has already received an email. Overall, this would not be an issue if we were not adjusting the tasks / pulses and assignee’s while defining the plan. We may even go through MULTIPLE iterations of the plan before we are ready for management review, causing many users to get emails (sometimes 30-50 a day) on tasks they may never ever be assigned to once the project kicks off. This is laborious when some of the projects we work on (this is medical, so LOTS of assigned tasks) can have 500+ pulses assigned.

Is there a way to turn off email notifications to the assigned users when creating a pulse? Especially as we’re deleting and adding pulses and defining the plan?

posts.author: rujs (Sjur Brekke)
posts.cookedHtml:
This would help us allot as well.

Also; it would be great if you can chose if users are notified based on changes in a group/pulse they are assigned to, and not every change on the whole board. The fact that you dont have more options regarding notifications is frustrating as it makes difficult to keep track of e.g. the development of the pulses (tasks) you are assigned to in boards with allot of groups and pulses

posts.author: straister (Shelly Traister)
posts.cookedHtml: No - unfortunately, I did not get an answer to this and it is causing us to use the application less then we’d like to see.

posts.author: lisamdye (Lisa Dye)
posts.cookedHtml: We could use this feature also.

posts.author: katrippon (Kat Rippon)
posts.cookedHtml: I could really benefit from this feature too

posts.author: TamaraLove (Tamara Love)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 vote for the draft feature - would be super handy!

posts.author: darren (Darren Wiebe)
posts.cookedHtml:
This, especially the last part that was added afterwards, would be very helpful for us.

There are lots of times when we want somebody tagged to a project but they don’t need to see it. For example, on my development projects board, we’ll be working on a project that was requested by a particular user but I can’t tag it to them without added them to a board that they have no business getting access to.

posts.author: r.bausch (Raphael)
posts.cookedHtml: This would be extremely essential also to my company. It’s very hard to plan projects and schedules in advance and test out different scenarios if people get notified all the time

posts.author: LauraV (laura vetro)
posts.cookedHtml:
straister:

ernative option might be - Allow us to use people on boards they are not subscribed to , and even as I add this - I see value for internally working on management level organizational data where people fields add value, but can’t be used because the people are not part of the leadership group need

I totally agree, too. The default-to-automated-email behavior has embarrassed me. I prefer that nothing automated should happen unless I intentionally set it up, especially emails and communications.

posts.author: straister (Shelly Traister)
posts.cookedHtml:
As a workaround (which we don’t love) , we now shut off all except the following notifications when we add new Monday users:
-tagged comments
-people forget to tag others to get an uodate
-automation notifications
-replies to a message you wrote

While this gives us a lot more control (to add users to boards without sending email), here are the drawbacks-
*incredibly tedious to set up users
*users can personally add notifications back, (and the board owner doesn’t know who did that. No way to stop this
*users can a still see the boards in the left panel and Monday inbox

posts.author: Elliot (Elliot Cryer)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have a potential work around that you could use.

Maybe not the best work around for you as it sounds like you have a lot of users but might help some of the smaller accounts.

  • Create a status column next to the people column while planning with all your users on it.
  • Duplicated the column for as many users you might need on a single item.
  • Change those status’s as many times as you need during planning.
  • When finalised, selected all the uses as indicated by the status next to them to send out notifications then delete the status column/s with the user names.

Setting up the status column every time would be tedious but you would only have to do it once if you are using a template board or if you are in enterprise there is a “save column as template” option.

I hope that helps you or somebody else with this issue.

Cheers,

Elliot.

posts.author: straister (Shelly Traister)
posts.cookedHtml:
That’s interesting. Not sure I can visualize it but we’re using the following workaround and it’s worked ok:

Shut off all notifications in the app for users during setup EXCEPT:
1- allowed from automations - automation for people column alerts can be set per board (purposeful) and yet can be muted during planning
2-tagged in a comment☁️
3-replies to comments (since many from email and can’t tag)

This has worked really well for now, giving a lot of control, but relies HEAVILY on users understanding how critical (both) tagging and the use of strategic automation driven notifications are.

(of course they technically can turn them back on)

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
It would be very helpful if the people column has a setting that says “Do not notify”. Then we could add users to this column while planning and when doen change the setting to “Notify now”. So my features request is to have a column setting for people column with these options.

  • Do not notify > will never notify people
  • Notify when added > current behavior
  • Notify now and for future changes > notifies all people once and then fall backs to default behavior

posts.author: Lwalker (Lindsey)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes! we have a lot of shifts in projects and dates – being able to resource plan and allocate the right person with a constantly shifting schedule before we trigger any action is critical. I’ve created columns as @Elliot suggested, but it’s far from optimal.

posts.author: squas (Paul Mullan)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is so infuriating. I want to add people who have requested my team to do tasks in my team’s board and roughly plan who in my team could own those tasks.

I added the requestors and team members and woke up to a barrage of Slacks and questions about what I had assigned to them. I wanted no notifications (at least until we discussed it in a planning session).

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
It would be so nice if we had an option on the people column that says “notify yes/no”. Set it to “no”, do your rough planning and when finished, switch it to “yes”. It would be even better if people assigned to tasks get notified:

  • when they are assigned while the setting is “yes” (as is, leading to multiple notifications)
  • just ONE notification for the complete boards when the option is flipped from “no” to “yes”, like: “you are assigned to 7 tasks in board xyz”

@Julia-monday.com any idea if this in on the roadmap?

posts.author: deltacx (Debbie L)
posts.cookedHtml: I want this too. I want to add people to plans and play around with who is assigned to what. I don’t want people getting these notifications while I’m still playing around with planning and resourcing. Thanks.

posts.author: BoazArad (Boaz Arad)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi all! Thanks for the feedback on this. I agree with all you said and happy to inform we are working on a solution to enable assigning users without automatically notifying them. No eta yet, we just started exploring this option but it is something we will address.
Updates to follow!
Thanks

posts.author: ElliBA (Elinor Bohlin Andersson)
posts.cookedHtml:
I wish there was a “like” or a “vote” button because this suggestion is so great. Good to hear that Monday.com is working on a solution, hopefully it will be avaialble soon. Good work @straister for sending this absolutely amazing feature suggestion!
CUDOS!

posts.author: straister (Shelly Traister)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thanks for chiming in @ElliBA - there is a voting button at the top of this page. I think a lot of people comment and like the comments/workarounds and some forget to vote. If you can, that would be great! This is still something the team struggles with, and thus we shut of notifications when we add new users, except when
1- allowed from automations - automation for people column alerts can be set per board (purposeful) and yet can be muted during planning
2-tagged in a comment☁️
3-replies to comments (since many from email and can’t tag)

they can add themselves back, but it has not been an issue so far and this (though painful and users can miss things where people forget to tag), has gotten us by

posts.author: straister (Shelly Traister)
posts.cookedHtml: thanks @BoazArad

posts.author: TamikaKennedyPM (Tamika Kennedy )
posts.cookedHtml: @BoazArad - does the solution include adding “dummy” users. For example, I want to track which tasks are tasked to “client” or tasks that include our whole team without actually assigning them, just for tracking purposes on the backend. Thanks!

posts.author: Kristinemd13 (Kristine Brooks)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m interested in adding people/names that aren’t users yet. I work in a shared services space that supports many internal groups of clients. We are unable to support the onboarding of all of our clients at one time & want to give our shared services folks the opportunity to pilot the process with a select number of users while not having to managed their work/process in two places (a board in monday & the previously managed spreadsheet).

posts.author: DylanDowns (Dylan Downs)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi all! Thanks for the feedback on this. I agree with all you said and happy to inform we are working on a solution to enable assigning users without automatically notifying them. No eta yet, we just started exploring this option but it is something we will address.
Updates to follow!
Thanks

Any update/ETA on this, Boaz?

posts.author: george_planit (George)
posts.cookedHtml: I’d like to be able to assign people (and guests) to tasks on a private staging board (i.e. so they can’t see it, get no notifications for) and then they are notified when the task moves to the active boards. I think this is in the same vein as this, unless there are other workarounds.

posts.author: Lara-S (Lara Steyn)
posts.cookedHtml: Glad to hear this! It’s really important for us with capacity planning.

posts.author: Vikram (Vikram Kanabarkar)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 for this feature request

posts.author: willcarterart (Will)
posts.cookedHtml:
@BoazArad 9 months since your last post, any updates?

I am keen for this feature also. I want to add my team into the platform to monitor all the workstreams we have. I intend to use it instead of my spreadsheet to keep track of tasks and be able to provide high-level updates to executives etc. on the fly. I have no intention of inviting any of my team to interact on the platform.

I am testing it at the moment through the trial version and this function will be the difference on whether I subscribe to Monday.com or look for an alternative.

Thanks, Will.

posts.author: Katey (kate@elevate.dev)
posts.cookedHtml: We were moving forward with using Monday for resource planning but may now scale back usage of Monday for this reason. We are unable to create private boards for resource allocation in the planning stage.

posts.author: Domyx (Kris)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 for this feature request

posts.author: Kristinemd13 (Kristine Brooks)
posts.cookedHtml: Is there an update on this? Is it slated on the roadmap?

posts.author: serx1111 (Robert Sparks)
posts.cookedHtml:
I am very interested in this solution. I would love to be able to add people top a task that are not linked to Monday.com. Clients or business partners my not want to be invited or want to log into Monday.com but there parts of a project still require tracking. this is a huge issue for this solution and should be fixed ASAP.

Also @BoazArad I think we are all on the edge of our seat for an update.

posts.author: Bev.Dunfield (Bev Dunfield)
posts.cookedHtml: We’d benefit from being able to disable notifications on a People column as well. Our use case is to record a list of communication recipients; they shouldn’t be notified that they’re on the Monday item when it’s originally assigned to them but should be available to use with ‘notify’ logic in automation.

posts.author: r.sironneau (Sironneau)
posts.cookedHtml: +1 for this important feature also. Any news about date availability ?

posts.author: bonnie.moore (Bonnie Moore)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi, any updates on this request? This would be super useful for us to track our new hires. We do not want notifications going out to them.

posts.author: ingridbee (Ingrid Berendt)
posts.cookedHtml:
I would like to have access to a board, but not automatically be subscribed to all notifications.

To give you more context, we use Monday boards to track company goals. There are dozens of items, and dozens of people chiming in daily on each one. It’s made my inbox basically useless for all the noise. If I unsubscribe from the board then I can no longer access to company goals, read about, or update on progress myself.

posts.author: Chaya (Chaya)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes! I have people on my team who complain that there are too many email notifications for things they dont need to see. If they shut notifications, they dont get what they need…they end up ignoring all Monday.com emails…spells disaster!
I need to be able to completely customize notifications not just a general idea…and only opt in to some, not all, when accessing a board

posts.author: deena (deenateichman)
posts.cookedHtml: I completely agree! I am new to Monday but finding the entire notification system non scientific and not smart for exactly the reasons you said. What I was told to do is basically turn all the email notifications off aside for the option for updates that @ mention you and teach my team to always @ mention in all updates and replies.

posts.author: Chaya (Chaya)
posts.cookedHtml: @deena that is a decent idea but not the most efficient…it means that an automation needs to be set up for every single thing that you DO want an update on…lots of detail in the set up...@monday.com should really work on this

posts.author: ingridbee (Ingrid Berendt)
posts.cookedHtml:
I think there is a lot of room for options and flexibility here. And I think we’re all asking for the same thing. The ability to easily pick and choose what we want to see updates for, down to the item level. And having visibility to a board should not be tied to also receiving notifications about it, unless I select that option.

Please upvote this thread if you want to support changes to the notifications.

posts.author: deena (deenateichman)
posts.cookedHtml: @Chaya i agree- any better ideas? curious what others do as a work around?

posts.author: Chaya (Chaya)
posts.cookedHtml:
@deena I am following your suggestion for now- removing ALL notifications and creating automations for the ones I want - its a huge pain and I need to create them in every single board I have open. I am open to better ideas as well…
@ingridbee is right, we need to keep upvoting, the Monday guys will work their magic to fix us up soon enough…

posts.author: deena (deenateichman)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes- Monday Community pls vote on this so we get attention on this. As aside, if any users have other creative workarounds, please share!

posts.author: stoppa (Gustavo Stoppa Alcantara Santos)
posts.cookedHtml: Concordo com o que @abelha está propondo. Estar vinculado a um quadro é importante, porém não quero receber todas as notificações dele.

posts.author: Johanna (Johanna Elliott)
posts.cookedHtml: +1. My company needs this as well. It would be a game changer to be able to shut off notifications for a specific board or workspace.

posts.author: MarkNariy (Mark McNairy)
posts.cookedHtml: I agree that notifications are crazy. I have created a lot of boards at my company as a super user and that automatically subscribes me to random thing that I have no concern with. Like I constantly get notifications that someone deleted something that I am subscribed to. I am only subscribed because I am the board owner. I don’t need to see any of that. Up vote!

posts.author: Lynsey (Lynsey McIver)
posts.cookedHtml:
Does this help anyone (profile > notifications)

image758×887 43.8 KB

posts.author: Ozi (Oz Israel)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello Ingrid,

Press your avatar at the lower left corner and go to “My Profile”, then go to the notifications section. Check or uncheck notification according to your preferences.
I hope this will solve the problem.

Good luck,
Oz

posts.author: ingridbee (Ingrid Berendt)
posts.cookedHtml: Appreciate those that are pointing me to the notification preferences, but that’s not the issue. The preferences don’t offer the option I am looking for. I have contacted Monday about this specifically and they agreed that the level of flexibility in the notifications is not currently available, which is why I made this post. I want to have access to a board without getting all the notifications related to that board.

posts.author: andreayenawine (Andrea Yenawine)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is definitely a huge issue. I am in the same boat as @MarkNariy in that I am the Admin for our team, and as such, create a lot of the boards, but I don’t necessarily participate in all of those boards. I end up getting the notifications from those boards and have to either remove myself as an owner or (in many cases) assign someone else as the owner so I can be removed as the owner. This is more annoying than difficult for me. However, we are a small company and do not have a lot of active boards at one time. I can see how this would be very challenging with a lot more boards.

For everyone’s sake, I hope the Monday team can get to this soon. I upvoted it a day or two ago and since then there has been about 8 more votes. So, for now, I guess the best is to continue to upvote as much as possible.

I, too, am open to any other work-arounds. I appreciate everyone’s participation and willingness to try to help one another on this thread.

Andrea

posts.author: bozday (Matt Bozek)
posts.cookedHtml: Agreed, this would be a great feature. I frequently look at boards where I take no action, and don’t need the additional notifications.

posts.author: jhamiter (Johnathan Hamiter)
posts.cookedHtml: Hoping this gets done, both for individual and Team assignments.

posts.author: Julietteb (Juliette )
posts.cookedHtml:
Congratulations. This feature has been released and our community had a part in its creation! You are now able to add users to boards without them automatically being notified.

Since this feature has been released we will be closing this thread. If you have any questions about the feature or want to share with us how you are using it, search for the feature in our product discussion section or create a new topic. We are looking forward to seeing how you implement these features into your workflows!

To learn more about the feature request process, how it works, and how to become part of the magic read here.
